title: Regime-Specialized Experts With Monotone Sigmoid Blend
summary: F1 strategy literature repeatedly highlights different behavior under dry vs wet tyres and under neutralization-like conditions because pit-loss economics and degradation dynamics change sharply.
rationale: F1 strategy literature repeatedly highlights different behavior under dry vs wet tyres and under neutralization-like conditions because pit-loss economics and degradation dynamics change sharply. That argues for separate specialists rather than a single global model, with monotone post-blending that preserves ranking.
implementation_hint: Define coarse regimes such as dry compounds, wet/intermediate compounds, and anomaly laps that proxy SC/VSC or traffic using extreme LapTime_Delta, lap gaps, and field-wide pace shocks. Train separate models per regime or a gating feature, then blend their OOF scores with logistic/sigmoid calibration only, not isotonic, to preserve AUC ranking.
expected_effect: Reduces regime interference and should improve robustness on rare wet or disrupted race segments.
risk: Regime labels are proxy-based and may be noisy; too many experts can fragment data and hurt stability.
